channel.title: USDJPY News Feed
channel.description: Latest USDJPY headlines from investingLive, ForexLive, ForexCrunch, ActionForex, Investing.com and DailyForex

item.title: [ActionForex] USD/JPY Rises for Sixth Straight Day: Yen Back on the Cusp of Intervention
item.description: USD/JPY climbed to 158.93 on Monday, marking the yen’s sixth consecutive session of decline. The Japanese currency is under pressure from a stronger dollar amid rising expectations that the Federal Reserve may raise interest rates this year to curb inflation. US inflation is accelerating due to the

item.title: [investingLive] What are the bias, risks, and key targets for the EURUSD, USDJPY, and GBPUSD right now?
item.description: As the new trading day gets underway, markets are off to a relatively cautious and choppy start. Oil prices are modestly lower and trading near session lows after giving back an earlier spike higher. U.S. yields are also slightly lower — down around 1 basis point across much of the curve after tradi

item.title: [ActionForex] USD/JPY Mid-Day Outlook
item.description: Daily Pivots: (S1) 158.35; (P) 158.59; (R1) 158.99; More… Intraday bias in USD/JPY remains on the upside at this point. Rise from 155.01 should target 160.71 high. But strong resistance is expected from there to start the third leg of the near term corrective pattern. On the downside, break of 157.3
